message:
mcp-ruqu v0.1 — HTTP transport (lifts mcp-rvdna pattern)

Adds Streamable HTTP transport on top of the v0.0.1 stdio-only tool
surface. The transport is lifted near-verbatim from mcp-rvdna::http
(commit a66d65f), including the iter 32 CORS layer (echo Origin,
expose mcp-session-id, short-circuit OPTIONS preflight) so the ruLake
Console can drive ruqu-mcp directly without a CORS proxy.

Changes
- Bump crate to 0.1.0; add ruqu-mcp binary (stdio + http subcommands).
- Default HTTP bind 127.0.0.1:7442 (rvdna-mcp owns 7441).
- AuthMode / BearerAuth / AllowBearerOnPublic / InsecureAllowNoAuth
  types plumbed through serve() so the v0.2 mTLS/JWT lift from
  mcp-server is mechanical.
- AuditSink::open_file() — JSONL file sink (was stderr-only in v0.0.1)
  so the --audit-file CLI flag works the same way it does on rvdna-mcp.
- mcp-ruqu/scripts/http-smoke.sh — iter 41 CORS contract (5 headers),
  MCP handshake, tools/list of 5 ruqu_* tools, RUQU_OPTIMIZE_STUB
  envelope check.
- scripts/smoke-all.sh — registers the new smoke as the fourth entry.

Tool semantics unchanged: ruqu_simulate / ruqu_verify / ruqu_replay /
ruqu_optimize / ruqu_qec_schedule are served identically over stdio
and HTTP. The five v0.0.1 unit + integration tests still pass; one new
http::tests::refuses_no_auth_on_public_without_override test guards
the loopback gate. Capability tiers remain plumbed-but-unused per
ADR-008 §6 Decision 4 (ship in v0.2 alongside the policy module
agreement with mcp-server).

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

@@ -1,40 +1,51 @@
-# ruQu MCP companion server (ADR-008 §6 v0.0.1).
+# ruQu MCP companion server (ADR-008 §6 v0.1).
 #
-# v0.0.1 scope (this PR):
-#   - Five tools wired via rmcp's `#[tool_router]` macro pattern,
-#     mirroring `mcp-server/src/server.rs`:
-#       * `ruqu_simulate`    (publish-tier — allocates Hilbert-space RAM)
-#       * `ruqu_verify`      (read-tier   — replays + byte-equal witness check)
-#       * `ruqu_replay`      (read-tier   — cache short-circuit via lake.search_one)
-#       * `ruqu_optimize`    (read-tier   — v0.0.1 stub; planner ships in v0.1)
-#       * `ruqu_qec_schedule`(read-tier   — v0.0.1 stub; QEC ships in v0.2)
-#   - JSONL audit emission with the same shape as mcp-server (ADR-004 §7).
-#   - R-1 mitigation: hard-refuse empty / overlong `circuit.id`.
-#   - R-2 mitigation: `enforce_qubit_cap` BEFORE `execute()`.
-#   - R-3: in-band documentation on `Rz` (theta not in witness in v0.0).
+# v0.0.1 shipped the five tools (rmcp `#[tool_router]` pattern):
+#   * `ruqu_simulate`    (publish-tier — allocates Hilbert-space RAM)
+#   * `ruqu_verify`      (read-tier   — replays + byte-equal witness check)
+#   * `ruqu_replay`      (read-tier   — cache short-circuit via lake.search_one)
+#   * `ruqu_optimize`    (read-tier   — stub; planner ships in v0.2)
+#   * `ruqu_qec_schedule`(read-tier   — stub; QEC ships in v0.2)
+# plus JSONL audit, R-1 / R-2 / R-3 mitigations, stdio transport.
 #
-# Out of scope for v0.0.1 (deferred to later mcp-ruqu releases):
-#   - HTTP transport, mTLS, JWT/JWKS, rate limiting, replay guard
-#     (mcp-server already provides those building blocks; we'll wire
-#      them in mcp-ruqu v0.1 when the planner stub turns real).
+# v0.1 adds (this release):
+#   - Streamable HTTP transport, lifted near-verbatim from
+#     `mcp-rvdna::http` (commit a66d65f) — same iter 32 CORS layer
+#     (echo Origin, expose `mcp-session-id`, short-circuit OPTIONS
+#     preflight). Default bind 127.0.0.1:7442 (avoids clash with
+#     rvdna-mcp's documented 7441 default).
+#   - `ruqu-mcp` binary with `stdio` / `http` subcommands matching
+#     `rvdna-mcp`'s CLI shape so a single operator script can drive
+#     both servers.
+#   - JSONL `--audit-file` sink (was stderr-only in v0.0.1).
+#
+# Out of scope for v0.1 (deferred to mcp-ruqu v0.2):
+#   - mTLS, JWT/JWKS, rate limiting, replay guard. The HTTP `serve()`
+#     signature already carries the `AuthMode` / `BearerAuth` /
+#     `AllowBearerOnPublic` / `InsecureAllowNoAuth` types so the
+#     v0.2 lift from `mcp-server::http` is mechanical.
 #   - The `simulate` / `hardware` / `verify` capability tier additions
 #     from ADR-008 §6 Decision 4 — capability set is plumbed but the
-#     v0.0.1 tools all gate on the existing `Read` / `Publish` tiers
+#     v0.1 tools still gate on the existing `Read` / `Publish` tiers
 #     so we don't fork the policy module before mcp-server agrees.
 #
 # Standalone Cargo package per ADR-001 (no root workspace; sibling
 # crates reach each other via path-deps; rulake from crates.io v2.2).
 
 [package]
 name        = "ruvector-rulake-mcp-ruqu"
-version     = "0.0.1"
+version     = "0.1.0"
 edition     = "2021"
 rust-version = "1.89"
 license     = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
 authors     = ["Ruvector Team"]
 repository  = "https://github.com/ruvnet/RuLake"
 description = "MCP companion server for ruQu v2 — agent-callable, witness-aware quantum execution surface (ADR-008 §6)"
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "ruqu-mcp"
+path = "src/main.rs"
+
 [dependencies]
 # The substrate this server wraps. Path-dep onto the sibling
 # `ruqu-backend/` crate per ADR-001; no workspace.
@@ -45,16 +56,27 @@ ruvector-rulake-ruqu = { path = "../ruqu-backend" }
 # byte-equality semantics. Match the version mcp-server pins.
 rulake = "2.2"
 
-# Official Rust MCP SDK. Same feature set mcp-server uses, minus the
-# HTTP transport — v0.0.1 ships stdio + the in-process router only.
-# `transport-streamable-http-server` lands when v0.1 wires HTTP.
+# Official Rust MCP SDK. Same feature set mcp-server / mcp-rvdna use,
+# now including `transport-streamable-http-server` for the v0.1 HTTP
+# transport.
 rmcp = { version = "1.5", default-features = false, features = [
     "server",
     "transport-io",
+    "transport-streamable-http-server",
     "macros",
     "schemars",
 ] }
 
+# Streamable HTTP transport stack — hyper 1.x (matches what rmcp uses
+# internally for `transport-streamable-http-server`). Mirrors mcp-rvdna
+# / mcp-server byte-identical so the workspace target/ stays single-
+# rooted when CI builds all three crates together.
+hyper = { version = "1", features = ["server", "http1", "http2"] }
+hyper-util = { version = "0.1", features = ["tokio", "server-auto", "service"] }
+http = "1"
+http-body-util = "0.1"
+bytes = "1"
+
 # Async runtime — matches mcp-server's pinned set.
 tokio = { version = "1.39", features = ["rt-multi-thread", "macros", "io-std", "signal", "sync", "net", "time"] }
 
@@ -63,8 +85,11 @@ serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }
 serde_json = "1.0"
 schemars = "1.0"
 
-# Structured logging — matches mcp-server.
+# Structured logging — matches mcp-server. `tracing-subscriber` only
+# ships with the binary entry point; the library leaves subscriber
+# choice to the caller.
 tracing = "0.1"
+tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter", "json"] }
 
 # Error type for callers; same idiom mcp-server uses.
 anyhow = "1"

@@ -0,0 +1,164 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+#
+# mcp-ruqu v0.1 — end-to-end HTTP smoke.
+#
+# Builds + launches ruqu-mcp on a free port, walks the MCP handshake
+# (initialize → notifications/initialized → tools/list), asserts the
+# response carries the five expected ruqu_* tool names, and asserts
+# that the `ruqu_optimize` stub returns the documented
+# `RUQU_OPTIMIZE_STUB` audit code over the wire. Tears the server down
+# on exit.
+#
+# Companion to mcp-rvdna/scripts/http-smoke.sh — same iter 32 + 41
+# CORS contract (5 preflight headers), same SSE-framed tools/list
+# parsing.
+#
+# Usage:
+#   ./mcp-ruqu/scripts/http-smoke.sh
+#
+# Exit:
+#   0 on green; 1 on any assertion failure or curl error.
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+CRATE_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
+BIND_HOST=127.0.0.1
+BIND_PORT=17442   # high port to avoid clashing with the default 7442
+BIND="${BIND_HOST}:${BIND_PORT}"
+URL="http://${BIND}/mcp"
+LOG=/tmp/ruqu-mcp-smoke.log
+
+ok()   { printf '\033[32m  ✓\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
+err()  { printf '\033[31m  ✗\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; FAILED=1; }
+hdr()  { printf '\n\033[1m▸ %s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
+info() { printf '    %s\n' "$*"; }
+
+FAILED=0
+
+cleanup() {
+  if [[ -n "${MCP_PID:-}" ]] && kill -0 "$MCP_PID" 2>/dev/null; then
+    kill -TERM "$MCP_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
+    sleep 0.3
+    kill -9 "$MCP_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
+  fi
+  pkill -9 -f ruqu-mcp 2>/dev/null || true
+}
+trap cleanup EXIT
+
+hdr "build"
+cargo build --release --manifest-path "${CRATE_DIR}/Cargo.toml" --bin ruqu-mcp 2>&1 | tail -2
+ok "ruqu-mcp built"
+
+hdr "launch"
+"${CRATE_DIR}/target/release/ruqu-mcp" http \
+  --bind "${BIND}" \
+  > "${LOG}" 2>&1 &
+MCP_PID=$!
+sleep 1.2
+if ! kill -0 "$MCP_PID" 2>/dev/null; then
+  err "ruqu-mcp died at launch — see ${LOG}"
+  cat "${LOG}" >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+ok "ruqu-mcp listening on ${BIND} (pid ${MCP_PID})"
+
+hdr "CORS preflight (iter 41 contract — Console at :4173 must be allowed)"
+# OPTIONS request mimicking a browser preflight from the Console.
+# v0.1 lifts mcp-rvdna's iter 32 CORS layer wholesale; this guards
+# against a regression that would otherwise only surface in the
+# cross-mcp browser smoke.
+PREFLIGHT=$(curl -isS -X OPTIONS "${URL}" \
+  -H 'Origin: http://localhost:4173' \
+  -H 'Access-Control-Request-Method: POST' \
+  -H 'Access-Control-Request-Headers: content-type, mcp-session-id')
+PRE_STATUS=$(echo "${PREFLIGHT}" | head -1 | awk '{print $2}')
+if [[ "${PRE_STATUS}" == "204" ]]; then
+  ok "preflight returned 204 No Content"
+else
+  err "preflight returned ${PRE_STATUS}, expected 204"
+fi
+for header in 'access-control-allow-origin: http://localhost:4173' \
+              'access-control-allow-methods:' \
+              'access-control-allow-headers:' \
+              'access-control-expose-headers: mcp-session-id' \
+              'access-control-max-age:'; do
+  if echo "${PREFLIGHT}" | grep -qi "^${header}"; then
+    ok "preflight carries: ${header%%:*}"
+  else
+    err "preflight missing header: ${header%%:*}"
+  fi
+done
+
+hdr "MCP handshake"
+INIT_RAW=$(curl -isS -X POST "${URL}" \
+  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
+  -H 'Accept: application/json, text/event-stream' \
+  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"smoke","version":"0"}}}')
+SESSION=$(echo "${INIT_RAW}" | grep -i '^mcp-session-id:' | head -1 | tr -d '\r' | awk '{print $2}')
+if [[ -z "${SESSION}" ]]; then
+  err "initialize did not return mcp-session-id"
+  echo "${INIT_RAW}" | head -10 >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+ok "session id received: ${SESSION:0:8}..."
+
+curl -fsS -X POST "${URL}" \
+  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
+  -H 'Accept: application/json, text/event-stream' \
+  -H "mcp-session-id: ${SESSION}" \
+  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}' > /dev/null
+ok "notifications/initialized accepted"
+
+hdr "tools/list (5 ruqu_* tools expected)"
+TOOLS_RAW=$(curl -fsS -X POST "${URL}" \
+  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
+  -H 'Accept: application/json, text/event-stream' \
+  -H "mcp-session-id: ${SESSION}" \
+  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"tools/list","params":{}}')
+# Response is SSE-framed; extract the JSON line that starts with `data:` then jq it.
+TOOLS_JSON=$(echo "${TOOLS_RAW}" | grep -E '^data: ' | grep -v '^data: $' | head -1 | sed 's/^data: //')
+if [[ -z "${TOOLS_JSON}" ]]; then
+  err "tools/list returned no SSE data line"
+  echo "${TOOLS_RAW}" | head -10 >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+EXPECTED=(ruqu_simulate ruqu_verify ruqu_replay ruqu_optimize ruqu_qec_schedule)
+for tool in "${EXPECTED[@]}"; do
+  if echo "${TOOLS_JSON}" | grep -q "\"name\":\"${tool}\""; then
+    ok "tool present: ${tool}"
+  else
+    err "tool missing: ${tool}"
+  fi
+done
+
+hdr "tools/call ruqu_optimize (stub → expect RUQU_OPTIMIZE_STUB in note)"
+# `ruqu_optimize` is a v0.0.1 stub (planner ships in v0.2). The
+# response envelope MUST carry `RUQU_OPTIMIZE_STUB` in the `note`
+# field — same string the audit row carries — so the wire and audit
+# stories agree. This proves the live tool path over HTTP without
+# requiring a backend / lake to be wired in.
+OPT_RAW=$(curl -fsS -X POST "${URL}" \
+  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
+  -H 'Accept: application/json, text/event-stream' \
+  -H "mcp-session-id: ${SESSION}" \
+  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":3,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"ruqu_optimize","arguments":{"circuit":{"n_qubits":2,"gates":[{"kind":"h","q":0}],"id":"smoke-optimize"}}}}')
+OPT_JSON=$(echo "${OPT_RAW}" | grep -E '^data: ' | grep -v '^data: $' | head -1 | sed 's/^data: //')
+if [[ -z "${OPT_JSON}" ]]; then
+  err "ruqu_optimize returned no SSE data line"
+  echo "${OPT_RAW}" | head -10 >&2
+elif echo "${OPT_JSON}" | grep -q 'RUQU_OPTIMIZE_STUB'; then
+  ok "stub envelope carries RUQU_OPTIMIZE_STUB"
+else
+  err "expected RUQU_OPTIMIZE_STUB in response, got: ${OPT_JSON:0:200}..."
+fi
+
+if [[ "${FAILED}" -eq 0 ]]; then
+  echo
+  printf '\033[32m\033[1m✓ mcp-ruqu http smoke green\033[0m — all 5 tools served, RUQU_OPTIMIZE_STUB path verified\n'
+  exit 0
+else
+  echo
+  printf '\033[31m\033[1m✗ mcp-ruqu http smoke RED\033[0m — see failures above\n'
+  exit 1
+fi

@@ -19,6 +19,11 @@
 #   3. mcp-rvdna/scripts/http-smoke.sh     — rvdna-mcp HTTP transport
 #                                             in isolation (handshake
 #                                             + 5 tools + refusal path).
+#   4. mcp-ruqu/scripts/http-smoke.sh      — ruqu-mcp HTTP transport
+#                                             in isolation (same iter 41
+#                                             CORS contract; 5 ruqu_*
+#                                             tools + RUQU_OPTIMIZE_STUB
+#                                             stub-envelope check).
 #
 # Usage:
 #   ./scripts/smoke-all.sh
@@ -57,6 +62,7 @@ fi
 SMOKES=(
   "${CONSOLE_LABEL}|${CONSOLE_SMOKE}"
   "rvdna-mcp HTTP|${REPO_DIR}/mcp-rvdna/scripts/http-smoke.sh"
+  "ruqu-mcp HTTP|${REPO_DIR}/mcp-ruqu/scripts/http-smoke.sh"
 )
 if [[ "${SKIP_CROSS}" -eq 0 ]]; then
   SMOKES+=("Console + mcp-rvdna cross-component|${REPO_DIR}/ui/scripts/smoke-cross-mcp.sh")
